# Vendor Management Standard

Owner: Mikhail Kurzin
Area: AI, InfoSec
Last edited time: January 20, 2026
Approvers: Mike Yan, Dmitry Kushnikov, Antony Gorin, Anastasia Shchogoleva, Dan McKelvey
Document ID: STD-IS-04
Document Type: Standard
Document application: All Contractors, All Employees
Effective date: December 12, 2022
Status: Published
Tag: ISO27001, ISO42001
Version: 4

<aside>

**Key points:**

- Vendor review
- Security Review
- Supply Chain
</aside>

<aside>

*This standard sets the key principles and requirements for addressing information security in the new vendor onboarding, implementation and offboarding process. Usage of SaaS service providers is associated with additional risks of data disclosures due to data processing in an uncontrolled environment. This document accumulates best practices in vendor security reviews to minimize the risks of Manychat data disclosure.*

</aside>

---

# Table of contents

---

# 1. General

## **1.1 Purpose**

The document describes the security aspects of relations with third-party vendors and suppliers to ensure that proprietary information, customer data and business systems are protected from unauthorized access (internal or external), use, disclosure, modification, or destruction (deliberate or accidental) throughout the information lifecycle. Also the document contains information and details regarding data and artifacts that should be provided within the process at each stage of supplier onboarding.

This standard also defines requirements for the onboarding, monitoring, and offboarding of third-party vendors that provide **AI systems, AI services, or components used within AI-enabled features**, ensuring alignment with Manychat’s Artificial Intelligence Management System (AIMS) and responsible AI objectives.

## **1.2 Scope**

This standard applies to all divisions, teams, and services of Manychat and all personnel of Manychat. It also applies to external organizations and their personnel who have been granted access to the Manychat infrastructure and services.

This standard applies to vendors that provide AI models, AI services, machine learning capabilities, or AI-enabled components that are integrated into Manychat’s products or operations, including third-party AI service providers.

The document is reviewed at least annually to ensure it meets the current business requirements of Manychat, partners, customers, and external organizations.

# **2. Vendor Management Standard**

## **2.1 Requesting a new vendor**

The process of vendor evaluation is required upon:

- New SaaS solution purchase.
- Third-party add-on or extension installation or purchase from approved apps marketplaces (e.g. Google Suite add-ons, Slack, Zoom, etc.).
- Standalone software installation with cloud synchronization functionality and ability to store corporate data in third-party cloud storages.
- SaaS, external API or on-premise software purchase for integration into Manychat product.
- Engagement of a vendor providing AI models, AI services, or AI-enabled functionality that is integrated into Manychat products or workflows.

The use cases of the selected solution shall be clear and understood. The functionality shall be compared to the tools already utilized in the Manychat environment (check [Systems access checklist](https://www.notion.so/Systems-access-checklist-cf4b67638ca54ac8987de1967c0d4586?pvs=21)).

## **2.2 Process description**

### 2.2.1 Vendor evaluation

New vendor purchase requests and renewals are conducted in accordance with internal flow. There following mandatory steps must be conducted for the new vendor onboarding:

- Initial vendor request;
- Manager and/or Department Head approval.
- Legal and Finance review;
- Security review (optional, see below);
- Final CEO approval ( for purchase >$5,000).

Security review is essential in the following cases:

- Software and/or SaaS subscription purchase;
- Internet/Telecom/Hosting suppliers purchase;
- Third party consulting company engagement with access to business systems or customer data;
- Analytical agencies engagement with access to customer data, PII, lead generation, or advertising.

![Screenshot 2025-12-04 at 1.38.28 PM.png](Vendor%20Management%20Standard/f77dcd4a-e450-4359-9e37-a1b5243163be.png)

[](data:image/png;base64,)

### 2.2.2 AI Vendor Evaluation

Where a vendor provides AI systems or AI services, the vendor evaluation shall additionally consider:

- the vendor’s role in the AI value chain (e.g. AI model provider, service provider, infrastructure provider);
- whether the vendor develops, trains, fine-tunes, or only operates AI models;
- the vendor’s responsible AI commitments, policies, or governance frameworks (where available);
- transparency regarding AI system capabilities, limitations, and change management practices;
- the potential impact of the AI service on individuals, customers, and Manychat’s business.

The depth of the evaluation shall be proportionate to the **criticality and impact** of the AI-enabled use case.

### 2.2.3 Initial vendor request

Requests shall be submitted by the employee via Manychat ServiceDesk App. The requests are assigned to the Finance Team and Security Team for review and approval.

Before the purchase the following details should be gathered by requester for the Finance Team review:

[Legal Process Flow](https://www.notion.so/Legal-Process-Flow-b6577a73119c49758f84e0b09afc0a4b?pvs=21) 

Before the purchase the following details should be gathered by requester for Security Team review:

| **Question** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **System description** | Description of the reason for selecting a new tool or contractor and its purpose. Use cases description is essential. This will help the Security Team to define the data confidentiality level. |
| **System Owner (Manager)** | The team that will own the system and the list of teams that will be using the system or working with contractor |
| ***Data Classification questions*** |  |
| **Will the system/contractor  get access to the clients personal data?** | The system/contractor might collect/process such data or integrate with another system, which is used for customer engagement |
| **Will the system/contractor get access to the clients content/subscribers?** | The system/contractor might collect/process such data or integrate with another system |
| **Will the system/contractor process sensitive information, such as financial data or PII?** | The system might collect/process such data or integrate with another system, which is used to process regulated information. In this case legal advisory is essential |
| ***Business level questions*** |  |
| **Will the system outage for more than 24 hours cause severe damage to your work?** | This helps to identify the system availability requirements |
| **Will the system outage for more than 24 hours cause severe damage to Manychat product operations?** | This helps to identify the system availability requirements |

The Security review questionnaire should be submitted under the following conditions:

- The employee wishes to purchase a SaaS;
- The employee plans to select a vendor/contractor to perform automated activities within company owned systems;
- The employee plans to attract third party consulting company with access to corporate infrastructure systems or customer data;
- The employee plans to attract analytical agencies with access to customer data, PII, lead generation, or advertising;
- The employee wishes to run a trial securely and ensure that the vendor fits security requirements. In this case security approval will be enough for trial purposes before the actual purchase.

### 2.2.4 Legal and Financial review

Legal and Finance reviews cover the confidentiality and finance aspects of the future agreement. Such review must include, but not limited to:

- Confidentiality statement clearance of a contract or end-user agreement;
- Mutual Non-Disclosure agreements for negotiation purposes;
- Intellectual property rights terms (if relevant);
- Data Protection Agreements (if relevant).

Legal and Finance review is conducted by the Finance Team in accordance with [Legal Process Flow](https://www.notion.so/Legal-Process-Flow-b6577a73119c49758f84e0b09afc0a4b?pvs=21) .

### 2.2.5 Security review

Security vendor/contractor screening is a mandatory part of the vendor/contractor selection. However, the depth of the review depends on the system requirements and data classification.

It is recommended to collect the following information for each system disregards its nature and purpose of use:

- Supported authentication methods;
- Access control mechanisms provided by the system;
- Password requirements;
- Ability to collect system logs;
- System Data Flow diagram.

For vendors providing AI services or AI-enabled functionality, the Security Team shall additionally assess, where applicable:

- documented AI security and safety controls;
- data handling responsibilities between Manychat and the vendor;
- mechanisms to prevent unauthorized data use or unintended data retention;
- availability of logging, monitoring, and incident notification related to AI service usage.

Additionally, the information shall be collected depending on the classification  level:

| **Classification** | **Additional artifacts** |
| --- | --- |
| [**Confidentiality](https://www.notion.so/Data-Classification-Policy-2bfb12e9aa1a809aa48bd02294c10582?pvs=21) and presence of customer data** |  |
| **Public** | No additional artifacts required.
In case if the system collects personal data of user accounts, Privacy Policy must be checked against usage of such data for analytics purposes. |
| **Internal**
**Confidential**
**Lead/Contract data**
**Customer-owned content** | • Results of independent security reviews (SOC2 reports/ISO27k certificates/CAIQ/SIG questionnaires) 
• Results of latest penetration test reports
• Public information security policy
Security team does not accept certifications released for other companies, e.g. hosting providers. The certifications must be up to date or cover the closest acceptable time period. |
| Additional artifacts for high availability requirements |  |
| Mission Critical | Public SLA
Disaster recovery plan
Incident response plan |

In custom cases, the Security team may request the vendor to complete an additional security questionnaire in addition with the requested information.

In case if the vendor can significantly impact the Manychat business objectives, Security Team has a right to enforce to sign an additional agreement addressing security of Manychat data or block the purchase in case of unacceptable risks occur.

### 2.2.6 Review deviations

In case if a selected vendor does not meet the security requirements, appropriate risks of usage must be assessed in partnership with the process owner for further final decision of the Head of Finance and CEO.

### 2.2.7 Periodic Vendor reviews

For Mission Critical systems annual security reassessments should be conducted due to expiry of security artifacts collected during initial review. The annual review must include at least the collection of evidence of independent security reviews, penetration test reports and updated policy versions.

In case if the security questionnaire was completed due to the absence of the artifacts mentioned above, the questionnaire must be completed during the next review cycle.

For AI service providers, periodic reviews shall also consider material changes to AI models, AI service behavior, contractual terms related to AI use, or changes in the vendor’s responsible AI practices.

### 2.2.8 Vendor implementation

Vendor onboarding shall be implemented with the support of Workplace Administrators in accordance with [Access Management Standard](https://www.notion.so/Access-Management-Standard-2b7b12e9aa1a800e84d4c113c69536d0?pvs=21) and [Office Security Standard](https://www.notion.so/Office-Security-Standard-2b7b12e9aa1a8005ab2edd693784d06f?pvs=21). Workplace Administrators must ensure that system is included in business system inventory, standard authentication methods (eg. Google SSO/Okta) are used, billing settings are set, security settings are enabled, standart protection solutions are installed (if applicable) and necessary logging level is configured.

### 2.2.9 Vendor offboarding

Vendor offboarding shall be implemented with the support of Workplace Administrators in accordance with [Access Management Standard](https://www.notion.so/Access-Management-Standard-2b7b12e9aa1a800e84d4c113c69536d0?pvs=21) and [Office Security Standard](https://www.notion.so/Office-Security-Standard-2b7b12e9aa1a8005ab2edd693784d06f?pvs=21). Workplace Administrators must ensure that system information is updated in business system inventory, billing settings are updated or canceled, all confidential information is backuped (if necessary) and wiped, and standard protection solutions are disabled (if applicable).

For vendors providing AI services, offboarding shall include confirmation of termination of AI service access and handling of AI-related operational data in accordance with contractual and retention requirements.

# **3. Roles and Responsibilities**

The CEO has the overall responsibility for Information Security at the Company, including information security regarding personnel and IT security.

Process specific roles and responsibilities:

| **Role** | **Role description** |
| --- | --- |
| **All employees** | Employees are responsible to comply with the Standard and report the violations to the Security team. |
| **Team Managers** | Team managers are responsible to ensure the awareness of this Standard across their teams. Additionally Team managers have a right to submit intakes to purchase a CSP solution through the buyer and must make sure that all steps are complete before the purchase. |
| **Leadership Team** | Leadership team is accountable for approvals of annual budget. Additionally Leadership team managers have a right to submit intakes to purchase a CSP solution through the buyer and must make sure that all steps are complete before the purchase. |
| **Security Team** | Security team is responsible for monitoring overall awareness with this Standard, monitoring, and investigating the violations, and deviations handling. Security team must make a decision in case a security review is necessary.
The Security Team is also responsible for assessing AI-related risks associated with third-party vendors and coordinating with AIMS stakeholders where AI services are involved. |
| **Finance Team** | Make the final decision on payment, such as issuing a payment order after making sure that all relevant approvals including due-diligence review are in place. |

# **4. Compliance**

The proof of management system effectiveness includes the receipt and maintenance of certification to **ISO/IEC 27001** and **ISO/IEC 42001**, where applicable.

Manychat must comply with multiple state regulatory requirements of countries, Manychat operates in, such as:

- The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) (Regulation (EU) 2016/679);
- Personal data protection regulation in the countries or regions of operation (including CCPA and Law of Armenia on protection of personal data ( ЗR-49-N));
- Canada’s Anti-Spam Law (or “CASL”);
- The CAN-SPAM Act;
- other mandatory state regulatory acts including acts on commercial secrets and intellectual property.

## 4.1 Enforcement

Implementation and enforcement of this policy is ultimately the responsibility of all employees at Manychat. Security team may conduct ad-hoc checks to ensure compliance with this document without notice. Any violation shall require immediate corrective action. Violations shall be noted in the Manychat applications and responsible employees shall be dispatched to remediate the issue.

For Manychat employees, breaches of this policy may result in disciplinary action being taken and will be dealt with under the disciplinary process in accordance with state laws and regulations of the residence country. Any serious breaches or repeated breaches of this policy may be deemed by Manychat as gross misconduct and may result in disciplinary sanctions, including a remark, reprimand, and dismissal from work.

## 4.2 Exceptions

Exceptions to this standard must be processed in accordance with the deviation handling rules by the Security team. All exceptions must be maintained taking into account associated risks, exceptions, and recorded approvals. Continuously detected deviations may trigger the changes or improvements of the measures defined in this document.
